title: PPL Ground School
intro: The full theoretical-knowledge path for the Saudi Private Pilot Licence — nine subjects, taught lesson by lesson by Captain Adel, ending at the written-exam mock test.

modules.title: Air Law & Regulations
modules.summary: The GACARs, airspace, the rules of the air and the route to a licence.

lessons.title: How Saudi aviation is regulated
lessons.objective: What GACA is, what the GACARs are, and how ICAO fits in.
lessons.adel: Captain, I'm starting PPL ground school. Teach me how civil aviation in Saudi Arabia is regulated — what GACA is, what the GACARs are, how they are numbered, and how they relate to ICAO. Keep it at student-pilot level.

lessons.title: Airspace classes and the Jeddah FIR
lessons.objective: The ICAO classes A–G and how Saudi airspace is structured.
lessons.adel: Captain, teach me the ICAO airspace classes A to G as adopted by GACAR — what each class requires of a VFR pilot — and how Saudi airspace is organised within the Jeddah FIR.

lessons.title: VFR rules — minima, altitudes, day/night
lessons.objective: VMC minima, cruising altitudes and the VFR limits of GACAR Part 91.
lessons.adel: Captain, teach me the VFR rules a Saudi PPL student must know — the VMC weather minima by airspace class, VFR cruising altitudes, and the rules on night and special VFR — with the GACAR Part 91 sections.

lessons.title: Licensing — the path to a PPL
lessons.objective: Age, medical, hours, exams and the skills test under GACAR Part 61.
lessons.adel: Captain, walk me through exactly what GACAR Part 61 requires to be issued a Private Pilot Licence — the age, the medical, the flight hours, the theoretical exams and the skills test.

modules.title: Meteorology
modules.summary: The atmosphere, weather, the hazards of flight and how to read a forecast.

lessons.title: The atmosphere & the ISA
lessons.objective: Structure of the atmosphere and the International Standard Atmosphere.
lessons.adel: Captain, teach me the basics of the atmosphere for PPL ground school — its layers, temperature and pressure with height, and the International Standard Atmosphere I'll use for performance.

lessons.title: Pressure, wind and altimetry
lessons.objective: How pressure drives wind, and QNH / QFE / standard settings.
lessons.adel: Captain, teach me pressure and wind for a student pilot — how pressure systems create wind, and altimetry: QNH, QFE, the standard setting and the transition altitude.

lessons.title: Cloud, visibility and flight hazards
lessons.objective: Cloud types, fog, thunderstorms, turbulence, icing and wind shear.
lessons.adel: Captain, teach me the weather hazards a VFR pilot in Saudi Arabia must respect — cloud types, reduced visibility and fog, thunderstorms, turbulence, icing, and low-level wind shear.

lessons.title: Reading METAR and TAF
lessons.objective: Decode an actual aviation weather report and forecast.
lessons.adel: Captain, teach me how to read a METAR and a TAF, field by field, using a Saudi aerodrome as an example.

modules.title: Navigation
modules.summary: Charts, dead reckoning, the flight computer and radio navigation.

lessons.title: Charts, projections and symbology
lessons.objective: How aeronautical charts work and how to read their symbols.
lessons.adel: Captain, teach me aeronautical chart basics for PPL ground school — chart projections, scale, latitude and longitude, and the common chart symbols.

lessons.title: Dead reckoning & the 1-in-60 rule
lessons.objective: Track, heading, drift and correcting with the 1-in-60 rule.
lessons.adel: Captain, teach me dead-reckoning navigation — track and heading, the wind triangle, drift, and how to use the 1-in-60 rule to correct a heading.

lessons.title: The flight computer (E6B)
lessons.objective: Wind triangle, true airspeed and time–speed–distance on the E6B.
lessons.adel: Captain, teach me how to use the E6B flight computer — the wind triangle for heading and groundspeed, true airspeed, and time–speed–distance problems.

lessons.title: Radio navigation and GNSS
lessons.objective: VOR, NDB, DME and satellite navigation in plain terms.
lessons.adel: Captain, teach me the basics of radio navigation for a PPL — VOR, NDB and DME — and how GNSS / GPS navigation works.

modules.title: Aircraft General Knowledge
modules.summary: The airframe, the engine, the systems and the instruments.

lessons.title: Airframe and flight controls
lessons.objective: The structure of the aeroplane and how the controls work.
lessons.adel: Captain, teach me aircraft general knowledge for a PPL student — the airframe structure of a light aeroplane and how the primary and secondary flight controls work.

lessons.title: The piston engine and propeller
lessons.objective: The four-stroke engine, mixture, carburettor icing and the propeller.
lessons.adel: Captain, teach me how a light-aircraft piston engine works — the four-stroke cycle, the mixture control, carburettor icing, and how the propeller produces thrust.

lessons.title: Aircraft systems
lessons.objective: Fuel, electrical, vacuum and pitot-static systems.
lessons.adel: Captain, teach me the main systems of a training aeroplane — the fuel system, the electrical system, the vacuum system and the pitot-static system.

lessons.title: Flight instruments
lessons.objective: The six-pack — how each instrument works and how it can fail.
lessons.adel: Captain, teach me the flight instruments — the six basic instruments, how each one works, and the typical errors and failures of each.

modules.title: Principles of Flight
modules.summary: Why an aeroplane flies — lift, drag, stability and the stall.

lessons.title: The four forces and lift
lessons.objective: Lift, weight, thrust and drag — and how a wing makes lift.
lessons.adel: Captain, teach me the principles of flight for a PPL — the four forces, and how a wing produces lift, including angle of attack.

lessons.title: Drag and the power curve
lessons.objective: Induced and parasite drag, and the speeds that matter.
lessons.adel: Captain, teach me drag and the power curve — induced versus parasite drag, the region of reversed command, and the key speeds like best glide and best climb.

lessons.title: Stability and control
lessons.objective: Longitudinal, lateral and directional stability.
lessons.adel: Captain, teach me aircraft stability and control — longitudinal, lateral and directional stability, and how the centre of gravity affects them.

lessons.title: Stalls, spins and angle of attack
lessons.objective: Why an aeroplane stalls, and how a stall becomes a spin.
lessons.adel: Captain, teach me about the stall — what really causes it (angle of attack), the symptoms and recovery, and how a stall can develop into a spin.

modules.title: Flight Performance & Planning
modules.summary: Weight & balance, performance, fuel and the flight plan.

lessons.title: Weight and balance
lessons.objective: Why CG matters, and how to compute it.
lessons.adel: Captain, teach me weight and balance for a PPL — why the centre of gravity matters, how to calculate weight and CG, and what happens if loading is outside the envelope.

lessons.title: Performance & density altitude
lessons.objective: Take-off and landing performance, and the effect of density altitude.
lessons.adel: Captain, teach me aircraft performance for a student pilot — take-off and landing distance, the factors that affect them, and how density altitude degrades performance on a hot Saudi day.

lessons.title: Fuel planning and reserves
lessons.objective: Trip fuel, contingency and the reserves a VFR flight needs.
lessons.adel: Captain, teach me fuel planning for a VFR flight — trip fuel, contingency, alternate and reserve fuel, and how to be sure I have enough.

lessons.title: Route planning and the flight plan
lessons.objective: Planning a cross-country and filing an ICAO flight plan.
lessons.adel: Captain, teach me how to plan a VFR cross-country flight in Saudi Arabia and complete an ICAO flight plan form.

modules.title: Human Performance & Limitations
modules.summary: The pilot as a system — physiology, health and decision-making.

lessons.title: Aviation physiology
lessons.objective: Hypoxia, hyperventilation, vision, hearing and disorientation.
lessons.adel: Captain, teach me aviation physiology for a PPL student — hypoxia, hyperventilation, vision and night vision, the ear and spatial disorientation.

lessons.title: Health, fitness and the medical
lessons.objective: Fitness to fly and the GACA medical certificate.
lessons.adel: Captain, teach me about fitness to fly and the GACA medical — what affects fitness to fly, common pitfalls like medication and fatigue, and what the medical certificate involves.

lessons.title: Decision-making and error management
lessons.objective: Airmanship, threat & error management and avoiding the traps.
lessons.adel: Captain, teach me aeronautical decision-making and threat & error management for a student pilot — the common hazardous attitudes and how to manage threats and errors.

modules.title: Operational Procedures
modules.summary: Normal, abnormal and emergency operations, and the aerodrome.

lessons.title: Normal procedures and airmanship
lessons.objective: Checklists, the traffic pattern and good operating practice.
lessons.adel: Captain, teach me normal operating procedures for a PPL — the use of checklists, the standard traffic pattern, and what good airmanship looks like.

lessons.title: Abnormal and emergency procedures
lessons.objective: Engine failure, fire, and handling an emergency.
lessons.adel: Captain, teach me how a PPL pilot handles emergencies — engine failure, fire, and the general principle of aviate, navigate, communicate.

lessons.title: Wake turbulence and wind shear
lessons.objective: Recognising and avoiding wake turbulence and wind shear.
lessons.adel: Captain, teach me about wake turbulence and low-level wind shear — how they form, where I'll meet them, and how to avoid or handle them.

lessons.title: Aerodrome operations and markings
lessons.objective: Runway and taxiway markings, signs, lighting and light signals.
lessons.adel: Captain, teach me aerodrome operations for a student pilot — runway and taxiway markings, signs, lighting, and the ATC light-gun signals.

modules.title: Communications
modules.summary: The radio, standard phraseology and language proficiency.

lessons.title: The radio and the phonetic alphabet
lessons.objective: How the radio works, frequencies and the ICAO alphabet.
lessons.adel: Captain, teach me aviation radio basics for a PPL — how VHF radio works, frequencies, the phonetic alphabet and how numbers are spoken.

lessons.title: Standard phraseology and the flight call
lessons.objective: Standard calls from start-up to shut-down.
lessons.adel: Captain, teach me standard radiotelephony phraseology — the typical calls a VFR flight makes from start-up, taxi and departure through to landing and shut-down.

lessons.title: English language proficiency (ELPT)
lessons.objective: The ICAO language requirement and the SAELPT.
lessons.adel: Captain, teach me about the ICAO English language proficiency requirement — the rating scale, the operational Level 4, and how the Saudi SAELPT test works.
